entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: The crop used to produce the flours for dough preparation

entities.name: TEXTURAL PROPERTIES
entities.description: The physical characteristics of the sorghum dough being evaluated

entities.name: DOUGH
entities.description: The mixture prepared from sorghum flours and water

entities.name: BACK-EXTRUSION CELL
entities.description: A tool used to measure the textural properties of the dough

entities.name: INSTRON FOOD TESTING INSTRUMENT
entities.description: The equipment containing the back-extrusion cell used for measurement

entities.name: FORCE
entities.description: The amount of effort required for the extrusion of the dough

entities.name: ENERGY
entities.description: The energy required for the extrusion of the dough

entities.name: COHESIVENESS
entities.description: The property of the dough to stick together, influencing its quality

entities.name: FLOUR-WATER-SOLUBLE COMPONENTS
entities.description: Components in the flour that affect the cohesiveness of the dough

relationships.description: Sorghum flours are used to prepare the dough
Dough is evaluated for its textural properties
Used to measure the textural properties of the dough
The instrument that houses the back-extrusion cell
Force is a measured parameter of textural properties
Energy is a measured parameter of textural properties
Cohesiveness is a specific textural property of the dough
These components influence the cohesiveness of the dough
